items.author: 0xocdsec

items.content:
RT @cr3ghost: BREAKING NEWS: Riot just announced Vanguard On-Demand. Vanguard no longer needs to start at boot.

If you enable Secure Boot, TPM 2.0, VBS, HVCI, and IOMMU, Vanguard will only run while you play. Microsoft built a Runtime Driver Attestation Report that lets Vanguard verify driver integrity without needing to be present at boot.

For everyone who complained about Vanguard running at startup, this is the answer. Enable your hardware security features and it goes away.

The blog also breaks down every cheat vector in detail: kernel exploits, BYOVD, DMA hardware cheats, bootkits, hypervisors, and pixelbots. With real data showing cheaters deliberately downgrade to older Windows to avoid security features.

https://t.co/qjuA7VJ34D

Some of the best security engineers in the industry working on one of the hardest problems in gaming. Massive respect for publishing this level of detail publicly.

@deteccphilippe @nickeverdox @aidankhoury 

#AntiCheat #Vanguard #GameSecurity
items.author: cr3ghost

items.content:
RT @cr3ghost: If you have ever wanted to learn reverse engineering but had no idea where to start, this is probably the best free series out there.

Applied Reverse Engineering by @daaximus covers basic architecture, the stack, exceptions and interrupts, x64 assembly, control-flow tracing, and more still coming including hooking techniques, ROP, heuristic analysis, and C++ class reconstruction.

Written for people who have opened a debugger a handful of times and got lost. Starts from zero and actually makes sense.

https://t.co/Cu40nKRBg5

#ReverseEngineering #WindowsInternals #InfoSec
items.author: cr3ghost

items.content:
RT @cr3ghost: Azeria Labs is probably the most complete free resource for learning ARM security research that exists.

ARM assembly from zero. Shellcode development. Stack overflows. ROP chains. Heap exploitation. iOS kernel heap grooming. TrustZone internals. GDB debugging. QEMU lab setup. Even an online ARM assembler.

If ARM exploitation is something you want to understand, start here.

https://t.co/MnfDCdNtrG
https://t.co/QtCZ3nhujT

Author: @Fox0x01 

#ReverseEngineering #ExploitDevelopment #InfoSec
items.author: cr3ghost

items.content:
RT @cr3ghost: If you want to learn malware reverse engineering and have no idea where to start, this is it. RE101 takes you from zero to reversing real Windows malware. RE102 covers anti-RE techniques, encryption, VM evasion, and packing. PE Injection Study walks through extracting process injection techniques from Cryptowall. macOS workshop covers Mach-O headers, code caves, and dynamic library injection.

Hands-on labs. Downloadable VMs. Real malware samples. IDA Pro cheat sheet included.

Free. No paywall. No signup.

https://t.co/mpbBMNJWrT

Author: @malwareunicorn 

#MalwareAnalysis #ReverseEngineering #InfoSec

Microcode and CTree API. Workflow optimisation. Debugging. Type libraries. Reversing C++ programs. AI-assisted reverse engineering. Plugin development.

From someone with 20+ years of IDA experience who helped build parts of the tool.

https://t.co/BQDHJoK6eG

Author: @allthingsida 

#ReverseEngineering #MalwareAnalysis #InfoSec

items.content:
RT @Unveiled_ChinaX: How does a single graduate school dropout single-handedly dismantle the elite academic hierarchy of a global superpower?

An explosive report by the Wall Street Journal reveals the incredible story of 33-year-old Geng Hongwei. Using basic statistical checks and AI tools, Geng produces viral short videos exposing blatant data fabrication in hundreds of biomedical papers. Most of these compromised studies were published in top international journals by the prestigious publisher Springer Nature.

Geng's targets are the crown jewels of the Chinese Communist Party's academic star system. He has exposed university deans, Yangtze River Scholars, and National Distinguished Young Scholars. Terrified of public social media backlash, Chinese universities have enacted swift punishments, ousting deans at elite schools like Tongji University and stripping titles far faster than typical U.S. bureaucratic processes. In response, multiple flagship Nature journals have been forced to launch official investigations.

However, exposing the truth under an authoritarian regime carries a terrifying cost. While millions praise Geng as a hero, online observers are deeply concerned for his personal safety. In a political climate hyper-focused on national image, uncovering state-funded academic fraud to the international community heavily risks being branded as "colluding with foreign forces."

#AcademicIntegrity #ChinaScience #SpringerNature #GengHongwei #CCP #ScientificFraud #Whistleblower 

https://t.co/CluugrCkUp

items.content:
🔍 𝗔𝗥𝗧𝗼𝗸𝗲𝗻 𝗗𝗲𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝘆

I’ve been dissecting multiple ARToken samples via https://t.co/LElYRAAzfB sandbox analysis. The lure often begins with a 𝗦𝗵𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗣𝗼𝗶𝗻𝘁 𝗱𝗼𝗰𝘂𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗸 — leveraging the trusted reputation of Microsoft’s cloud — before redirecting victims to phishing sites.

To counter this, I’ve crafted a 𝗠𝗶𝗰𝗿𝗼𝘀𝗼𝗳𝘁 𝗗𝗲𝗳𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗢𝗳𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗲 (𝗠𝗗𝗢) 𝗱𝗲𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 designed to surface suspicious links tied to ARToken infrastructure. This approach helps defenders flag malicious redirects hidden behind seemingly legitimate SharePoint lures.

ARToken: Inside an EvilTokens affiliate panel targeting Microsoft 365
https://t.co/lLIntlAX5B

#Cybersecurity #ARToken #PhaaS #Threathunting

items.content:
X censorship is insane and full of false positives.

@nikitabier. Is posting hoodie pics are illegal on X ?

In the security community we are getting far too many false positives with posts are locked and even suspensions because of the post. We’re not interested in politics or yapping about other people. We use X to learn skills and share quality research & blogs to learn. 

There’s clearly something broken at X right now. Please investigate and resolve this issue. otherwise, quality researchers will inevitably shift to other platforms.

Thank you ! Please share this as much as possible so it can reach the @X Head of Product @nikitabier and he can resolve the issues.

NOX is a modular attack surface management and vulnerability scanning framework written in Go with 299 built in modules covering OSINT, subdomain enumeration, DNS, port scanning, web fingerprinting, and deep active security testing across web, APIs, cloud, authentication, authorization, client side, and business logic.

Built for security researchers, penetration testers, and anyone who wants a fast, extensible platform for offensive security.

Feedback, bug reports, and contributions are always welcome.

items.author: 0day_ninja

items.content:
RT @ylecun: Exactly. I've been disseminating a similar message for years. 

The concentration of power in AI and the desire for control is by far the biggest danger of AI. It could lead to a few private companies and/or countries being in control of access to information, access to knowledge, and access to the tools of economic expansion. 
It's a kind of medieval obscurantism akin to the Ottoman empire banning the use of the printing press for 200 years, in part to keep control of the dogma, but also to protect the corporation of the calligraphers and scribes.

Relevant historical bits about the Internet:
1. It took a deliberate decision by Al Gore and Bill Clinton to open up access of what was then ARPAnet to commercial entities and to the public, against the desires of the entrenched telecom industry. During a public roundtable about the "information superhighway" in 1993, the CEO of AT&T told Gore and Clinton "leave it to us". Gore said no.
2. In the late 1980s, setting up an Internet presence required buying proprietary hardware with proprietary OS and software stack from Sun Microsystems, HP, IBM, or Dell. By the 2000s, all of this was wiped out by commodity hardware, Linux, Apache, and an entirely free/open software stack. This migration to open platforms was the result of market forces.

Infrastructure wants to be open.
Foundation models are becoming an infrastructure and will inevitably become commoditized.
Long term, the money is in the application layer, which is what I, Arthur Mensch, Alex Karp, and others have been saying.

items.content:
RT @nexta_tv: The Girl with the Snake Tattoo: Who is the mysterious killer behind the Monaco explosion?

A 39-year-old Ukrainian citizen, Anastasiia Berezovska, is suspected in the assassination attempt on oligarch Yermolaev. Interpol has issued a warrant for her arrest.

According to the investigation, she entered the businessman’s residence and planted an explosive device, which later detonated in the building’s entrance.

During the attempted assassination, she was dressed in a black jacket and a bucket hat, fully disguising herself as a man.

Three people were injured — Yermolaev himself, his wife, and a child.

Interpol has released a wanted notice describing the suspect as a woman with a tattoo on her arm and warning that she may be armed and dangerous.

Her whereabouts are currently unknown. The native of Luhansk region is believed to have left by car, first heading to Beausoleil in France, then to Italy, though she may already have reached the Balkans.

According to media reports, she previously lived in Germany as a refugee. German police have carried out searches in her apartment and vehicle.

items.content:
🪙 𝗔𝗥𝗧𝗼𝗸𝗲𝗻 𝗨𝗻𝗺𝗮𝘀𝗸𝗲𝗱

ARToken, first documented by Cisco Talos, is a phishing‑as‑a‑service panel linked to EvilTokens that abuses Microsoft 365 OAuth device codes to steal session tokens. With 80+ dashboard functions, it enables persistent access, BEC, and SharePoint/OneDrive exfiltration for affiliates.

The ARToken kit mirrors Microsoft’s device code authentication flow — including the identical 𝙿𝙾𝚂𝚃 /𝚊𝚙𝚒/𝚍𝚎𝚟𝚒𝚌𝚎/𝚜𝚝𝚊𝚛𝚝 request previously tied to EvilTokens. Using https://t.co/LElYRAAzfB Intelligence Lookup, defenders can track ARToken sites issuing these API calls and hunt for them in the 𝗥𝗲𝗺𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗨𝗿𝗹 field of the DeviceNetwork schema.🫡

https://t.co/lLIntlAX5B

#Cybersecurity #ARToken #PhaaS #Threathunting
items.author: 0x534c

items.content:
RT @Unveiled_ChinaX: By offering AI chips at just 1/4 of Nvidia’s price, state-backed Chinese giant Huawei is launching an aggressive offensive to hijack South Korea's semiconductor market.

According to Lianhe Zaobao, Huawei will launch its Ascend 950 series AI chips and Atlas 950 SuperPod in South Korea during Q4 2026. This cutthroat pricing directly targets the market by undercutting US competitor Nvidia, the global leader in AI hardware.

Huawei claims its inference-optimized Ascend 950PR chip delivers 2.87 times the performance of Nvidia's H20 GPU at a fraction of the cost. The full rollout features the 950PR, the training-focused 950DT, and the Atlas 950 SuperPod, an integrated infrastructure capable of linking up to 8,192 fully interconnected chips using Lingqu 2.0 technology.

Huawei's South Korean branch has already secured local distribution agreements with partners, including tech security firm SK Shieldus, to handle marketing, pricing, and training.

However, this state-subsidized hardware push faces major hurdles. Industry observers warn that South Korea's deep sensitivity toward Chinese core technologies and rising geopolitical tensions could derail Huawei's ambitions.

#Huawei #Nvidia #AIChips #Semiconductors #TechWar #Geopolitics #SouthKorea

items.content:
RT @RFGroenewoud: On Linux, a process can lie about its own name.

"exec -a '[kworker/0:2]' ./miner &"

...and it shows up in ps as a kernel worker thread. Real malware does this to hide in plain sight. Here is the detail that gives it away. https://t.co/NSTzi7N66e

items.author: Jean_Maes_1994

items.content: The real point here is that when you self-host open-weight models (Chinese ones like DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, or others) on your own GPUs or a US/EU data center, your prompts and data generally stay with you during inference. They don't automatically get sent back to the model's creators in China. That's different from feeding everything into OpenAI or Anthropic APIs, where data leaves your environment (even if enterprise plans often include promises of no training use, zero retention options, and data ownership).
items.author: 0day_ninja

items.content:
Ngl the selective "safety and security" framing does feel inconsistent at times. Companies routinely trust US providers with sensitive data under contracts and US law, while treating Chinese-origin open models as radioactive by default. Cost differences are massive also; like 10-50x+ cheaper for comparable performance, and Chinese open models have closed the capability gap dramatically so far. Many are competitive (or ahead) on coding, reasoning, and efficiency benchmarks. 

Just my $0.02

RT @DanielPriestley: Is it possible that the massive outrage Gary and his crew feel is down to a complete misunderstanding of how tax on a wealthy individual works? When I spoke to Gary, it certainly appeared that way… 

Gary was unaware that: 
- there a different types of tax that layer on top of each other. 
- all the same taxes that apply to an individual also apply to a  Duke. 
 - theres a huge difference between taxing individuals, their companies, the revenues they make or the value of their assets. 
- income and asset values are different things.

items.author: virusbtn

items.content:
RT @co11ateral: Nezha RMM Can Evade Detection

Often RMMs are abused for persistence and remote access by hackers. One of these apps is Nezha. 

Unlike AnyDesk for example, Nezha is much more subtle. Unlike AnyDesk, Nezha is much more subtle. It doesn't show up in the system tray. Since it uses preconfigured installation scripts for its agents, the deployment process can also be customized

Yet, it remains undetected. 

To spread awareness and share forensic artifacts we made our article on Nezha.

Learn more: https://t.co/8np8jXJkF0 

Other RMMs to monitor and block: https://t.co/CtFNVxjnlM

@three_cube @_aircorridor

items.content:
RT @GenThreatLabs: Most RATs have 2, maybe 3 stages. SilverFox's #ValleyRAT runs eight — and the last one is a kernel rootkit that takes orders from the RAT over named pipes. Campaign is live right now.

Kill chain in full: DLL sideload → ETW/AMSI bypass + PNG stego (payload hidden in pixel channels) → PrivEsc + more PNG stego → nested Donut shellcode → ValleyRAT orchestrator → Go RAT over WebSocket/QUIC → AV killer injected into svchost → kernel rootkit (65+ IOCTLs, boss'd around by the RAT via named pipes).

On top of that: crypto clipboard hijacking, Telegram theft, plugin delivery over named pipes.

13 polymorphic samples recompiled per victim over 12 days. Paths rotate daily under C:\Drivers. Delivered via trojanized installers abusing legit signed EXEs.

Active across 🇨🇳 🇭🇰 🇸🇬.

IoCs:
 520304a1cabdd9aa05c0a769c3874bc3cc2608d8e71ae607ca2bdf96b298b5de — trojanized installer
3w[.]jxuw3[.]com — WS C2
df[.]sjickdeh[.]org — WS C2

#SilverFox #APT #Malware

items.content:
RT @bridgemindai: FABLE 5 CAME BACK NERFED.

We re-ran the July 1st version of Claude Fable 5 on BridgeBench. 

The results are brutal:

Debugging: 86.2 → 25.9
Refactoring: 73.6 → 38.4
Hallucination: 75.9 → 61.7

The new guardrails are kicking in on way too many tasks and falling back to Opus 4.8.

This is not the model that got banned. 

Anthropic owes everyone an explanation.

items.content:
RT @Pirat_Nation: Sony’s stock rose more than 3% after the company announced it will stop releasing new PlayStation games on physical discs starting in January 2028.

Apparently, investors see the move as a way for Sony to increase profits, as selling games digitally eliminates the costs.

Sony said nearly 80% of full-game sales on PS4 and PS5 are already digital, up from 13% when the PS4 launched in 2013.

I don't think they will walk back on this decision even with all the backlash.
